title: quercus pyrenaica f. normalis
intro: Quercus pyrenaica f. normalis, also known as the Pyrenean oak, is a variant of the Pyrenean oak tree (Quercus pyrenaica) that has been cultivated for its valuable timber and medicinal properties. This plant is a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula, with a long history of use in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus pyrenaica f. normalis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and its timber is also highly valued for furniture-making and construction.
